title: Agent messaging: shared async inbox via message records
description: Give parallel agents a shared async inbox without a sidecar service, chat server, or external tool — messages live in the ledger they already use, inheriting durability, timestamps, forkability, and after-the-fact inspectability (spec §6.10). A coder posts step updates; an audit agent polls verdicts. Complements handoffs (a one-shot baton pass) with ongoing back-and-forth.
acceptance: Message record type per spec §6.10: id, thread (a task/issue id OR the reserved `project` folder-wide channel), from_agent, to_agent (nullable=broadcast), kind (update|question|verdict|note), body, in_reply_to, created_at.
Chat is scoped per project folder (one .waystation ledger = one message space); no cross-project inbox. A `project` channel carries folder-wide messages in addition to task/issue threads.
Messages are immutable and one file per record under .waystation/messages/ (lowest merge friction for parallel branches); no edit/delete.
`message post` writes through the single core write path (lock + atomic write) and appends a message.posted event.
`message list --thread <id>` shows a thread; `inbox --agent <a> [--since <cursor>] [--json]` returns messages addressed to the agent or broadcast on threads it holds a claim on.
The inbox is a query over records (a per-agent cursor, not a mutation). NOTE: served in-memory today; moves to the SQLite index when reindex covers messages.
bun:test covers posting, project-channel + claimed-thread broadcast, own-message exclusion, and inbox filtering by recipient + since-cursor.
DEFERRED to the MCP phase: expose post_message and get_inbox as MCP tools over the same core logic.
DEFERRED to task-error-envelope: return message results/errors via the CommandResult envelope.
notes: Beads stores comments in its Dolt DB; Waystation's git-committed JSON files are the equivalent (durable, forkable, inspectable). Keep it async and file-based — no real-time/presence/streaming, which would pull toward the deferred daemon and the 'do not become a chat tool' non-goal (§3). Uses the error envelope from task-error-envelope once that lands.
